Description: Rule R1 defines supplier master data and vendor setup capabilities in procure-to-pay. Users matched to this rule can create or maintain supplier records that downstream processes rely on for purchasing and payment.
Rule R2 covers invoice capture, validation, and posting activities. It identifies users who can initiate or advance supplier invoices through financial workflows prior to payment.
Rule R3 governs payment execution, release, and critical disbursement controls. It flags entitlements that allow a user to approve or trigger outbound payments.
Rule R4 relates to revenue recognition and sales order operations. Users under this rule can influence customer-facing order or billing flows that affect reported revenue.
Rule R5 captures credit limit, credit hold, and related commercial decision rights. It is used to detect combinations of access that could override normal credit governance.
Rule R6 represents operational directory or IAM support tasks such as routine provisioning requests. It distinguishes day-to-day support from full administrative control.
Rule R7 identifies highly privileged identity and directory administration entitlements. These capabilities can broadly change accounts, groups, or platform security settings.
Rule R8 covers roles or entitlements that allow a user to assign application or enterprise access to others. It is a key control point in access governance and SoD.
Rule R9 reflects approval or certification authority over access requests or role changes. Users mapped here can approve escalations or exceptions in access workflows.
Rule R10 includes recruiting, candidate, and hiring-process entitlements. It is evaluated when SoD policies concern separation between hiring actions and downstream HR maintenance.

Rule_Name: Worker Termination and HR Maintenance Access
Description: Rule R11 covers worker lifecycle maintenance including termination, status changes, and sensitive HR record updates. It is contrasted with recruiting or payroll rules in SoD scenarios.
Rule R12 defines payroll calculation, run, and adjustment capabilities. It is used where SoD requires separation between HR master data changes and payroll processing.

Rule_Name: HCM Platform Administration and Data Movement Access
Description: Rule R13 represents broad HCM platform administration, configuration, or data movement privileges. These entitlements can impact multiple tenants of worker and payroll data.
